project.title: Identification of ASB1 interacting proteins by LC-MS/MS
project.projectDescription: To identify proteins that interact with ankyrin repeat and SOCS box-containing 1 (ASB1) in mouse testes, immunocoprecipitation (IP) experiments were performed and subjected to two independently LC-MS/MS analysis and Label-free quantification.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Anti-ASB1 antibody (Proteintech) was incubated with total testicular lysates overnight, followed by the addition of 20 µl of washed protein A/G beads (Santa Cruz Biotechnology) and incubated for another 3 h at 4 °C. Beads were then pelleted and washed three times in 20× bed volume of lysis buffer. The bound protein was eluted by heating the beads at 95 °C for 5 min with 2× SDS buffer and then separated by SDS-PAGE. The gel particles were cut into blocks approximately 1 mm3 in size for in-gel digestion. Gel particles were first washed with deionized water, 50% acetonitrile (can), and 100% ACN. Next, the proteins were reduced and alkylated for 45 min at room temperature in the dark. The gel particles were then thoroughly washed, dried naturally, rehydrated at 4 °C for 30 min, and subjected to protein digestion at 37 °C for 12 h. Trifluoroacetic acid (TFA), at a final concentration of 0.1%, was added to stop the digestion, and the supernatants were transferred to fresh tubes. All extracts and supernatants were combined and dried in a SpeedVac. The digested peptides were desalted with StageTip and analyzed using an LTQ Orbitrap Velos mass spectrometer.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The MaxQuant software (version 1.3.0.5) was used for searching the raw files against UniProt mouse proteome database. The FDR of identified peptides and proteins was set to 1%. Searches were performed using Trypsin/P enzyme specificity while allowing up to two missed cleavages. Carbamidomethylation of cysteine residues (+57.0215Da) were set as fixed modifications. Variable modifications included oxidation of methionines and acetylation of protein N termini. The relative protein abundance ratios between the two groups were calculated based on LFQ by MaxQuant.
